channel.title: Java on Kampong-Abapagar Modern ABAP
channel.description: Recent content in Java on Kampong-Abapagar Modern ABAP

item.title: What Would Aristotle Think of Your Code?
item.description: Let’s be honest. Most of us have never read Aristotle. We’ve been too busy arguing about whether tabs or spaces are the one true path to enlightenment. But bear with me for a few minutes, because it turns out that a Greek philosopher who died in 322 BC had some surprisingly useful things to say about object-oriented design — and, inadvertently, about why Uncle Bob got one argument slightly wrong.
